rulings.Case Title: RONALD ANTHONY YATES VS. KATHRYN R. DEVINCENZI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Referees Final Report Of Sale Of Real Property Located At 228 Collins Street Pursuant To Joint Stipulation And Interlocutory Judgment For Partition Dated 8-19-2021; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, Approval Of Referee Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 20, 2022 line 1. NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR REFEREE'S FINAL REPORT OF SALE OF REAL PROPERTY LOCATED AT 228 COLLINS STREET PURUSNA TO JOINT STIPULATION AND INERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT FOR PARTITION DATE 8-19-2021 is GRANTED, in part, subject to any overbid. Pursuant to the August 13, 2021 Order, all costs and fees are to be determined by motion at the hearing for Entry of Judgment and attributed to Defendant in the final judgment. Referee's request for fees and costs is premature. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL ET AL VS. EILEEN L. WALSH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Approval Of Receivership Plan And Receivers Borrowing Pursuant To Receivers Certificate
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 20, 2022 line 2. RECEIVER'S MOTION FOR APPROVAL OF RECEIVERSHIP PLAN AND RECEIVER'S BORROWNING PURSUANT TO RECEIVER'S CERTIFICATE is GRANTED. The Receiver is authorized to market and sell the property known as 200 Judson Avenue, San Francisco, in its "as is" condition to a qualified buyer who will promptly undertake the rehabilitation. The Receiver is authorized to borrow up to $225,000 on behalf of the receivership estate and to issue and record a second Receiver's Certificate of Indebtedness to the lender in the amount borrowed. The Receiver is authorized to execute any documents necessary to effectuate the terms of this Order. No opposition filed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
